message:
[LOW] fcp-core: enforce sequence monotonicity on RevocationRegistry::update_head

Drops the `const` qualifier on update_head and short-circuits when `seq <=
self.head_seq` (for any non-initial head). Prior behavior unconditionally
overwrote the head pointer, so a stale/replayed checkpoint could roll the
frontier backwards — is_fresh(remote_seq) would then flip from true to
false against identical gossip, and freshness-SLA checks would restart
from the rewound timestamp.

Note: my earlier-staged revocation tie-break + idempotency tests landed
via concurrent commit a14273cd; this commit carries the update_head diff
only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
